boundary: This report classifies readiness only. It does not mark PRD checkboxes and does not replace generated evidence, deployed proof, or explicit user approval.

json.purpose: Source-only evidence for Aura Clash launch GLBs, playable fighter delivery, provenance, license, thumbnails, bounds, material readability, route usage, and character validation fields needed by Aura Clash showcase doc without editing the PRD.

sourcePolicy.typedAssetRule: Launch routes use typed Aura3D assets from src/aura-assets.ts and model(assets.*); string asset ids and unsafe model URLs are not accepted for launch proof.
sourcePolicy.licenseRule: Use official Quaternius staged assets only. The local package license text records CC0 1.0 Universal / Public Domain Dedication; Aura Clash still credits Quaternius as project policy.
sourcePolicy.fallbackRule: Playable fighters use clean typed GLBs for V1. No stylized primitive fighter fallback is approved in this evidence file; a missing fighter GLB should fail the production asset gate instead of silently substituting primitives.
sourcePolicy.visualGateRule: This file records source validation evidence only. Browser screenshot and human visual approval remain separate launch gates.

coverage: Every public launch GLB registered in aura.assets.json is represented below with provenance, license note, thumbnail, bounds, material summary, and intended route usage.
Every playable fighter record uses deliveryMode=typed-glb and fallbackApproval=none, with production evidence tied to check-production-assets.mjs and aura.assets.json.
Every playable fighter record includes source validation evidence for scale, pivot policy, facing policy, bounds, material readability, and thumbnail source.
The combined playable scene GLB is documented as a poster/evidence asset; the live playable route composes the duel stage and selected fighter typed GLBs at runtime.

typedQuaterniusFighterGlbs.proofBoundary: This is a source-level typed GLB proof contract. Browser screenshot quality, human visual approval, deployment, and live GLB URL reachability remain separate gates.

requiredBeforePrdCheck: executed browser/runtime screenshot evidence showing the Quaternius-derived fighters are visible, grounded, correctly oriented, readable, and not detached
human visual review remains separate from machine source metadata
deployment and live route/GLB URL reachability evidence remain separate deploy gates
quaterniusDerivedFighterValidation.proofBoundary: Source metadata is complete for gates 264/265 source readiness, but visual validation proof must remain unchecked until browser screenshots and human approval exist.
sourceReadyAssertions: six Quaternius-derived playable fighter GLBs are present in assets/source/fighters and registered as typed Aura assets
all fighter records include provenance, license note, source archive hash, bounds, material summary/material names, thumbnail evidence, and route usage
all fighter records keep fallbackApproval=none and approvedPrimitiveFallbacks=[]
live route source uses typed assets.* references and model(assets.auraClashDuelStage) composition

machineRequirements: all required captures return successful HTTP status
all required captures are nonblank according to imageEvidence.nonblank
visualReviewEvidence covers debug overlays, readable fighters, effects, HUD, stage depth, lighting, and materials
screenshotCapture.proofBoundary: A nonblank screenshot and source declarations can support review, but they do not complete visual quality approval without explicit human approval.

requiredReviewAreas: readable fighter silhouettes from key/rim light separation
intentional material contrast for Quaternius fighters and downtown stage surfaces
controlled bloom/fog/shadows without hiding HUD or fighters
three distinct screenshot compositions: match start, combat impact, super move/result

launchGlbs.name: Mara Volt
launchGlbs.licenseNote: Derived from the official Quaternius Universal Base Characters Standard archive staged locally; package license text records CC0 1.0 Universal / Public Domain Dedication, with Quaternius credit retained by project policy.
launchGlbs.materialSummary: Five named stylized readability groups: eyes, skin, suit, cyan aura accent, and boots; the typed manifest records the material slots and no external texture dependency.
intendedRouteUsage: /playable/ default player fighter and character-select option
/accessibility/ same playable route with accessibility controls
/evidence/ typed GLB registry and provenance proof
/poster/ represented in the combined playable-scene poster asset

productionEvidence.manifestEvidence: aura.assets.json records source, fingerprinted public output, hash, sizeBytes, bounds, materials, and thumbnailUrl.

sourceValidationEvidence.poseSource: Melee_Hook at actionFrame 0.50, frozen to mesh before export.
sourceValidationEvidence.pivotPolicy: Source builder exports with export_apply=true after pose freeze; runtime seats the GLB from its origin on the combat lane and foot-ring markers, so pivot validation source is the GLB origin plus positive Aura manifest bounds.
sourceValidationEvidence.facingPolicy: Source builder normalizes imported fighter objects with rotation_euler.z=180 degrees; runtime mirrors combat facing with route-level Y rotations.
sourceValidationEvidence.boundsEvidence: Aura manifest bounds are positive and within the fighter production size gate.
sourceValidationEvidence.materialReadabilityEvidence: Eyes, skin, suit, accent, and boots remain separate named slots; cyan/blue accent creates readable player identity against the neon stage.
sourceValidationEvidence.thumbnailEvidence: Autogenerated Aura thumbnail SVG is registered at /aura-assets/fighterMaraVolt.thumb.svg and covered by the production asset gate.
sourceValidationEvidence.launchRouteUseEvidence: Source route files map assets.fighterMaraVolt into the live /playable/ runtime composition; no primitive fighter fallback is approved.

launchRouteUse: /playable/ live selectable fighter through src/main.ts fighterAssets map
/playable/ typed route source references assets.fighterMaraVolt
/accessibility/ uses the same playable route composition with accessibility controls
/evidence/ exposes typed GLB registry, provenance, bounds, materials, thumbnails, and source validation fields
/playable/ default player fighter and character-select option
/accessibility/ same playable route with accessibility controls
/evidence/ typed GLB registry and provenance proof

playableFighterEvidenceSummary.productionEvidence: All playable fighters are required assets in apps/aura-clash-showcase/scripts/check-production-assets.mjs and typed entries in apps/aura-clash-showcase/aura.assets.json.
playableFighterEvidenceSummary.fallbackApprovalPolicy: approvedPrimitiveFallbacks is empty; each fighter requires fallbackApproval=none - clean typed GLB required.

launchRouteUseEvidence.composition: The live route composes assets.auraClashDuelStage with selected fighter typed GLBs at runtime. The combined auraClashPlayableScene GLB is poster/evidence proof and is not the live playable route.
routeUseSignals: src/main.ts maps each fighter assetKey to assets.<assetKey> and renders live route proof copy for /playable/.
src/scenes/createFighterNodes.ts maps each fighter assetKey to typed assets.<assetKey> before model() composition.

gateSourceReadiness.scope: docs/project/aura-clash-showcase.md gates 264/265 source readiness for Aura Clash asset provenance/source validation only.
sourceAssertions: Every playable fighter is a typed GLB with assetKey, typedAsset, sourcePath, publicUrl, hash, sizeBytes, bounds, materials, thumbnailUrl, provenance, and license note.
Every playable fighter has source validation fields for scale, pose, pivot, facing, bounds, material readability, thumbnail evidence, and fallbackApproval.
approvedPrimitiveFallbacks is empty and no primitive fighter fallback is approved for production launch proof.
The live launch route source composes assets.auraClashDuelStage plus selected assets.fighter* GLBs instead of string ids or unsafe model URLs.
This evidence intentionally does not claim browser screenshot quality, human visual approval, deployment, or live URL reachability.
gateSourceReadiness.proofBoundary: Source evidence can make gates 264/265 source-ready, but final visual/deploy gates remain pending until the required runtime artifacts exist and are reviewed.

launchRouteUseEvidence.composition: The live route composes assets.auraClashDuelStage with selected fighter typed GLBs at runtime. The combined auraClashPlayableScene GLB is poster/evidence proof and is not the live playable route.
routeUseSignals: src/main.ts maps each fighter assetKey to assets.<assetKey> and renders live route proof copy for /playable/.
src/scenes/createFighterNodes.ts maps each fighter assetKey to typed assets.<assetKey> before model() composition.

gates.prdLabel: Quaternius-derived fighter visual validation proof remains pending until `apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/first-frame.json`, `apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/first-frame.png`, `apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/review-package.md`, `apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/visual-approval.json`, and `apps/aura-clash-showcase/assets/source/aura-clash-launch-asset-evidence.json` prove the fighters are visible, grounded, correctly oriented, readable, and free of detached hair/clothes/accessories in runtime/browser screenshots.
gates.label: Quaternius-derived fighter visual validation proof: fighters visible, grounded, oriented, readable, and no detached accessories.
gates.commandPath: npm run launch:proof, then review launch-evidence/review-package.md, then record visual approval after explicit user approval.

json.purpose: Source-only evidence for Aura Clash launch GLBs, playable fighter delivery, provenance, license, thumbnails, bounds, material readability, route usage, and character validation fields needed by Aura Clash showcase doc without editing the PRD.

sourcePolicy.typedAssetRule: Launch routes use typed Aura3D assets from src/aura-assets.ts and model(assets.*); string asset ids and unsafe model URLs are not accepted for launch proof.
sourcePolicy.licenseRule: Use official Quaternius staged assets only. The local package license text records CC0 1.0 Universal / Public Domain Dedication; Aura Clash still credits Quaternius as project policy.
sourcePolicy.fallbackRule: Playable fighters use clean typed GLBs for V1. No stylized primitive fighter fallback is approved in this evidence file; a missing fighter GLB should fail the production asset gate instead of silently substituting primitives.
sourcePolicy.visualGateRule: This file records source validation evidence only. Browser screenshot and human visual approval remain separate launch gates.

coverage: Every public launch GLB registered in aura.assets.json is represented below with provenance, license note, thumbnail, bounds, material summary, and intended route usage.
Every playable fighter record uses deliveryMode=typed-glb and fallbackApproval=none, with production evidence tied to check-production-assets.mjs and aura.assets.json.
Every playable fighter record includes source validation evidence for scale, pivot policy, facing policy, bounds, material readability, and thumbnail source.
The combined playable scene GLB is documented as a poster/evidence asset; the live playable route composes the duel stage and selected fighter typed GLBs at runtime.

typedQuaterniusFighterGlbs.proofBoundary: This is a source-level typed GLB proof contract. Browser screenshot quality, human visual approval, deployment, and live GLB URL reachability remain separate gates.

requiredBeforePrdCheck: executed browser/runtime screenshot evidence showing the Quaternius-derived fighters are visible, grounded, correctly oriented, readable, and not detached
human visual review remains separate from machine source metadata
deployment and live route/GLB URL reachability evidence remain separate deploy gates
quaterniusDerivedFighterValidation.proofBoundary: Source metadata is complete for gates 264/265 source readiness, but visual validation proof must remain unchecked until browser screenshots and human approval exist.
sourceReadyAssertions: six Quaternius-derived playable fighter GLBs are present in assets/source/fighters and registered as typed Aura assets
all fighter records include provenance, license note, source archive hash, bounds, material summary/material names, thumbnail evidence, and route usage
all fighter records keep fallbackApproval=none and approvedPrimitiveFallbacks=[]
live route source uses typed assets.* references and model(assets.auraClashDuelStage) composition

machineRequirements: all required captures return successful HTTP status
all required captures are nonblank according to imageEvidence.nonblank
visualReviewEvidence covers debug overlays, readable fighters, effects, HUD, stage depth, lighting, and materials
screenshotCapture.proofBoundary: A nonblank screenshot and source declarations can support review, but they do not complete visual quality approval without explicit human approval.

requiredReviewAreas: readable fighter silhouettes from key/rim light separation
intentional material contrast for Quaternius fighters and downtown stage surfaces
controlled bloom/fog/shadows without hiding HUD or fighters
three distinct screenshot compositions: match start, combat impact, super move/result

sourceValidationEvidence.poseSource: Melee_Hook at actionFrame 0.50, frozen to mesh before export.
sourceValidationEvidence.pivotPolicy: Source builder exports with export_apply=true after pose freeze; runtime seats the GLB from its origin on the combat lane and foot-ring markers, so pivot validation source is the GLB origin plus positive Aura manifest bounds.
sourceValidationEvidence.facingPolicy: Source builder normalizes imported fighter objects with rotation_euler.z=180 degrees; runtime mirrors combat facing with route-level Y rotations.
sourceValidationEvidence.boundsEvidence: Aura manifest bounds are positive and within the fighter production size gate.
sourceValidationEvidence.materialReadabilityEvidence: Eyes, skin, suit, accent, and boots remain separate named slots; cyan/blue accent creates readable player identity against the neon stage.
sourceValidationEvidence.thumbnailEvidence: Autogenerated Aura thumbnail SVG is registered at /aura-assets/fighterMaraVolt.thumb.svg and covered by the production asset gate.
sourceValidationEvidence.launchRouteUseEvidence: Source route files map assets.fighterMaraVolt into the live /playable/ runtime composition; no primitive fighter fallback is approved.

playableFighterEvidenceSummary.productionEvidence: All playable fighters are required assets in apps/aura-clash-showcase/scripts/check-production-assets.mjs and typed entries in apps/aura-clash-showcase/aura.assets.json.
playableFighterEvidenceSummary.fallbackApprovalPolicy: approvedPrimitiveFallbacks is empty; each fighter requires fallbackApproval=none - clean typed GLB required.

launchRouteUseEvidence.composition: The live route composes assets.auraClashDuelStage with selected fighter typed GLBs at runtime. The combined auraClashPlayableScene GLB is poster/evidence proof and is not the live playable route.
routeUseSignals: src/main.ts maps each fighter assetKey to assets.<assetKey> and renders live route proof copy for /playable/.
src/scenes/createFighterNodes.ts maps each fighter assetKey to typed assets.<assetKey> before model() composition.

gateSourceReadiness.scope: docs/project/aura-clash-showcase.md gates 264/265 source readiness for Aura Clash asset provenance/source validation only.
sourceAssertions: Every playable fighter is a typed GLB with assetKey, typedAsset, sourcePath, publicUrl, hash, sizeBytes, bounds, materials, thumbnailUrl, provenance, and license note.
Every playable fighter has source validation fields for scale, pose, pivot, facing, bounds, material readability, thumbnail evidence, and fallbackApproval.
approvedPrimitiveFallbacks is empty and no primitive fighter fallback is approved for production launch proof.
The live launch route source composes assets.auraClashDuelStage plus selected assets.fighter* GLBs instead of string ids or unsafe model URLs.
This evidence intentionally does not claim browser screenshot quality, human visual approval, deployment, or live URL reachability.
gateSourceReadiness.proofBoundary: Source evidence can make gates 264/265 source-ready, but final visual/deploy gates remain pending until the required runtime artifacts exist and are reviewed.

launchRouteUseEvidence.composition: The live route composes assets.auraClashDuelStage with selected fighter typed GLBs at runtime. The combined auraClashPlayableScene GLB is poster/evidence proof and is not the live playable route.
routeUseSignals: src/main.ts maps each fighter assetKey to assets.<assetKey> and renders live route proof copy for /playable/.
src/scenes/createFighterNodes.ts maps each fighter assetKey to typed assets.<assetKey> before model() composition.

gates.prdLabel: Source manifests and typed asset declarations do not complete visual screenshot approval, deployed GLB reachability, or human visual quality approval; completion requires `first-frame.json`, `first-frame.png`, `review-package.md`, `deployed-routes.json`, and `visual-approval.json` with `ok: true`.
gates.label: Source manifests and typed assets are separated from screenshot approval, deployed GLB reachability, and human visual approval.
